subject: Mathematics
title: Cantor's Diagonal Argument
description: A quick, practical guide to Cantor's Diagonal Argument.

hook.text: Cantor devised one of the most elegant proofs in all of mathematics—using a simple diagonal trick.

buildup.text: Assume you can list all real numbers between 0 and 1. Write them in rows as infinite decimals.

discovery.text: Go down the diagonal: take the first digit of row 1, second of row 2, and so on. Change each digit.

twist.text: The new number differs from every row in at least one digit. It can't be on the list.

climax.text: No list can contain all real numbers. Uncountable infinity is proven in just a few lines.

punchline.text: One diagonal line demolished the idea of one-size infinity.

quiz.question: What does Cantor's diagonal argument prove?
options: The real numbers cannot be listed completely
